clusters.headline: Spain vs Argentina FIFA World Cup final: Team news, prediction and lineups
clusters.summary: Stars Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal clash at the biggest match of the biggest World Cup in history.

clusters.headline: Annual leave for outsourced Progressive Wage Model workers to increase from 7 to 10 days progressively from 2029
clusters.summary: About 60 per cent of outsourced workers in sectors such as cleaning, security and landscape are expected to benefit from the increase, says the Ministry of Manpower.

clusters.headline: Which Singapore celebrities own cafes? 35 stars who have gone into F&B, from Ben Yeo to JJ Lin and Rebecca Lim
clusters.summary: Running a restaurant isn't easy, even if you're famous. From Ben Yeo's ever-evolving restaurant empire to Taufik Batisah, The Muttons and Jeanette Aw, here's a roundup of the local celebrities who've opened everything from hawker stalls to cafes, steak restaurants and online food businesse

clusters.headline: Nigeria: Ododo Reaffirms No-Ransom Policy As Security Forces Rescue Abducted Neco Candidates in Kogi
clusters.summary: [Leadership] Kogi State governor, Alhaji Ahmed Usman Ododo, has reiterated his administration's policy of not negotiating with kidnappers or paying ransom, following the successful rescue of four persons abducted from a NECO examination centre in Dekina Local Government Area of the state.

clusters.headline: Congo-Kinshasa: In Eastern DR Congo, Journalists Face Death, Arson Threats for Reporting On War
clusters.summary: [CPJ] Government allied Wazalendo militiamen have threatened to kill or capture one reporter and to burn down another's radio station because of unfavorable coverage, as frontlines shift under diplomatic pressure for peace in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo.

clusters.headline: Rescuers find five survivors and are still looking for 20 others after boat sinks in Indonesia
clusters.summary: The five, including a 7-year-old girl, were found clinging to a fish trap belonging to a local fisherman, Makassar search and rescue officials said.

clusters.headline: Central Africa: Gabon's Public Debt Rises 23 Percent As Government Turns to Regional Markets
clusters.summary: [Daba Finance] Gabon's outstanding public debt rose 23% year-on-year to 8,780.3 billion FCFA at the end of December 2025, driven by a sharp increase in domestic borrowing as the government shifted away from traditional external financing.

clusters.headline: Deschamps takes responsibility for unacceptable first half in last game in charge of France
Uganda: Kapchorwa Bus Tragedy - 23 Ndejje Pupils Feared Dead After School Tour Turns Fatal
clusters.summary: [New Light] Kapchorwa: At least 23 pupils of King David Junior School Ndejje are feared dead following a road accident in Kapchorwa District on Thursday evening, as the pupils travelled for a field trip whose exact destination remained unclear at the time of the crash.

clusters.headline: Uganda: Anthony Natif Notes From Court - Uganda Vs Molly Katanga and Others July 16/2026 B
clusters.summary: [Independent (Kampala)] The last few weeks in the Katanga case have been about getting to the bottom of DNA evidence and its implications on understanding whether Mrs Molly Katanga and/or her deceased husband pulled the trigger that ended Mr Katanga's life on the morning of 2 November 2023.

clusters.headline: Nigeria: Nigerians Want Cheaper Petrol, but Renewed Hormuz Battle Won't Make That Happen
clusters.summary: [Premium Times] Many Nigerians clamoured that petrol prices should return to the pre-war level.

clusters.headline: 'Manosphere' influencer Andrew Tate and brother arrested in Miami, UK seeks extradition
clusters.summary: Andrew Tate is one of the most prominent proponents of the so-called "manosphere" network of communities, many of them online, that focus on traditional masculinity, anti-feminism, and self-improvement.
